column1: Category 1 - PFAS in Rice Tissues (Tier-1 BAF / calibration targets)
Concentrations & bioaccumulation/transfer factors paired with soil and/or porewater. Prioritised: Asian (Korea/China) paddy field & controlled paddy studies.

column8: flooded paddy; Andosol (volcanic ash, high OC); field
column15: South Korea; PFCAs dominant, PFOA most abundant. Best Korea-specific paired dataset.
Void water = porewater analog; the C_free driver for root uptake.
Brown rice > white (polished) rice. Grain = terminal accumulator in your model.
White rice PFOA = ND. Use with paired soil/porewater to derive PFOA grain TF.
PFOA(brown rice) correlated with PFOA(porewater) and PFDA(soil) -> supports porewater-driven (C_free) uptake; constrains f_xy.

column12: Mapping Legacy & Emerging PFAS in China staple crops, 2025 (ES&T)
column15: Grain median > soil median -> BAF>1 for short-chain PFCAs. First nationwide paired soil-grain set.

column2: BAF Sigma-PFAS, edible parts (range across crops)
column8: flooded/upland mix; FIP dose gradient 0.3 & 10 km; field
column15: log(BAF) decreases LINEARLY with PFCA chain length. Shoot vegetables highest; grains lower.
Quantitative slope for your grain-BAF chain-length QSPR. Liu 2019 (verified) reports the same linear trend qualitatively.

column5: crop C4-C7 ~14x PFOA; soil PFOA ~9.4x Sigma(C4-C7)
column15: Strong evidence short-chain anions translocate; long-chain retained in root. Key for f_xy(chain length).
OBS (Cl-free novel) predominant in rice; PFBA/PFOA dominate other grains. Low-background reference level.

column8: greenhouse paddy pot, 5-month full cycle, 10-100 ug/g dosing
column12: Fate/bioaccum/tox of PFOA,PFOS,GenX in paddy soil-rice, 2026 (J. Hazard. Mater.)
column15: GenX shows STRONGER upward translocation than PFOA/PFOS. Only controlled dose-response paddy-rice study found; key for TF ordering & replacements.

column5: long-chain retained in root; short-chain to shoot
column15: >4500 BAFs / 45 PFAS / 24 studies. BAF ordering root > straw > grain; vegetative > storage. Grain BAF falls up to ~1.16 log/CF2.
Best Poaceae SURROGATE for rice translocation where rice-specific compartment data is missing.

column8: Nakdong River basin; irrigation water 0.026-0.112 ug/L
column12: Nakdong River exposure study, 2020 (Environ. Geochem. Health)
column15: Korea context (not rice grain specifically). Irrigation water as a dominant exposure route - relevant to paddy water input.
